title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run in 4.1 innings as the Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Cardinals.
Early struggled with command, throwing 86 pitches and handing the game to the bullpen in the fifth inning.
The Red Sox dropped to a 4-9 record with a 1-6 road record after Friday's loss to St. Louis.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta has played 75 games with 74 starts, making a strong case for NBA's Most Improved Player award.
Queta averages 25.5 minutes per game and has recorded 17 double-doubles and 100 blocks this season.
It has been two decades since a center won NBA's Most Improved Player, with Queta emerging as a top candidate.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox struggle against MLB's Dustin May, generating only one hit from their top four batters in the lineup.
Calls for lineup changes grow as Wilyer Abreu and Masataka Yoshida show potential to improve early batting performance.
Prospects Mickey Gasper and others in Worcester considered for call-ups to address lineup inefficiencies.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs by defeating the New Orleans Pelicans 144-118.
Celtics tied the NBA's single-game three-point record with 29 made three-pointers for the second time in franchise history.
Boston aims for its 12th consecutive playoff appearance, the NBA's longest active streak, starting April 18.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Pizza Movie', starring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone, is now streaming on Hulu.
'Sirat', an Oscar-nominated film from 2025, is available for streaming on Hulu.
'The Boys' Season 5, featuring Antony Starr as Homelander, is streaming on Prime Video.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will not play in the Celtics' game against the Pelicans due to right Achilles repair management.
Jaylen Brown, who is managing left Achilles tendinitis, will be available for the game despite previous uncertainties.
The Celtics aim to secure the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, needing a win or a Knicks loss.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's cancer journey.
Robertson's daughter Emma was diagnosed with medulloblastoma in April 2025, requiring immediate surgery and extensive treatment.
Emma's treatment included relocation to Philadelphia for proton radiation due to maintenance at the Boston facility.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not debut for the Bruins against Tampa Bay on Saturday as the team seeks a playoff spot.
Coach Marco Sturm is taking a cautious approach with Hagens, emphasizing the need to integrate him into the system.
Hagens participated in Friday's practice on a 'fifth' line and earned reps on the second power-play unit.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers are three Boston-area billionaires listed on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans'.
The historic Forbes list includes Boston-linked figures like Benjamin Franklin, Sheldon Adelson, Babe Ruth, and Ted Williams.
Forbes lists determine rankings by financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact with 'Self-Made Scores'.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will occur on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year's marathon features 30,000 runners from over 130 countries, organized with six start waves for efficiency.
Live coverage of the marathon will be broadcast on WCVB Channel 5 and streamed via the Very Local app.
